title: parsed_jd.json
description: Structured metadata extracted from the raw posting (the workspace's jd.txt), for NAMING, TRACKING, and IDENTITY (recognizing a re-tailored posting), plus the keyword lists that ops/keyword_check.py greps for the deterministic keyword gate. It is NOT an input to the draft or fix agents; those must read the raw jd.txt. Written once by jd-parse when the application is first tailored; never rewritten on reuse; never blocks a run. The posting's URL is NOT a field here: it is code-owned end to end (the staging namespace's url file -> new_workspace.py -> the workspace's one-line .run/posting_url file).

id.description: The applications/<id>/ folder name, authored by code (ops/new_workspace.py): YYYY-MM-DD_<kebab(company)>_<kebab(role_title)>, truncated and collision-suffixed. The parser copies the folder's basename; it never invents an id.

company.description: Hiring company as named in the JD; null if not stated.

role_title.description: Role title as named in the JD; null if not stated.

date_parsed.description: ISO date (YYYY-MM-DD) the JD was parsed. Code-stamped from the clock, not model-authored.

mode.description: Work mode. Use "unspecified" when the JD does not state it; never guess.

sponsors_h1b.description: true = sponsors, false = explicitly does not, null = not stated.

keywords.description: The JD's hard requirement terms, in the JD's EXACT spelling: technologies, methods, and domain nouns a recruiter's ATS would string-match (e.g. "REST APIs", "CI/CD", "Go"). Broader than the stack fields (which stay technology-only). Copy each term verbatim from the posting; never normalize ("Go" stays "Go", not "Golang"). Read by ops/keyword_check.py for the deterministic keyword report; NOT fed to the writer.

required_stack.description: Required technologies as named in the JD. Extracted keywords for tracking, NOT a contract and NOT fed to the writer.

preferred_stack.description: Preferred/nice-to-have technologies. Extracted keywords for tracking, NOT a contract and NOT fed to the writer.

salary_range.description: As stated in the JD; null if not stated.

years_required_min.description: Minimum years of experience stated (the low end of any range); null if not stated.
